metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the README fenced-block extraction mechanism (which blocks run, what tool/script extracts them, how multi-block READMEs are handled), and add `b55f` (quickstart-repair) as an explicit `depends_on` in ticket 0435 so the gate is never wired before the hard-coded `reel-lot-tea` alias is removed.
findings.finding: The plan's core AC — 'the golden-path CI gate runs the literal commands extracted from the README quickstart fenced block' — asserts a novel parse-and-exec mechanism that does not yet exist in the repo (no script in scripts/ implements README fenced-block extraction), does not specify which of the three fenced blocks (bash, python, json) are executed, and does not specify the extraction tool or approach. Additionally, the bash fenced block currently contains `rebar claim reel-lot-tea` — a hard-coded alias that will not exist in a throwaway fresh repo — meaning the gate would fail for the wrong reason if wired before the quickstart is repaired. The child ticket for this gate (0435/contained-dendritic-angora) has an empty deps list, so no hard sequencing guard prevents the gate from being wired before b55f lands.
findings.location: AC section: 'The golden-path CI gate runs the literal commands extracted from the README quickstart fenced block' / Drift-proofing mechanism section / child ticket contained-dendritic-angora (0435) deps=[]
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new script (e.g. `scripts/run_readme_quickstart.sh`) that: (1) extracts only the first `bash` fenced block from `## Quickstart` in README.md using `awk`/`sed` or a Python helper, (2) runs it in a fresh `mktemp -d` repo, and (3) is invoked from the 0435 CI job. Add `depends_on: [b55f]` to ticket 0435 so CI wiring can only land after the quickstart is repaired to use a dynamically-created ticket id rather than the hard-coded alias.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add depends_on f088 to both 9d81 and 6755, or restate their ACs to say 'passes the docs-index CI gate once it lands (see f088)' so reviewers know the gate is a prerequisite for final verification.
findings.finding: Children 9d81 and 6755 each have an AC requiring they 'pass the docs-index CI gate (sibling ticket f088)' but neither declares a dependency on f088. If f088 is not yet implemented when 9d81 or 6755 are reviewed, their AC cannot be verified — and if f088 is merged after them, an intermediate state could exist where the gate does not yet enforce the index completeness that 9d81/6755 claim to deliver.
findings.suggested_fix: Make f088 a prerequisite for closing 9d81 and 6755 by adding depends_on f088 to each, consistent with how b5a4 explicitly depends_on e866 and 235a.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit depends_on a41f and b55f to child 0435, or add a note in 0435 that it must land after the README is repaired.
findings.finding: Child 0435 (golden-path CI) has no declared dependency on children a41f or b55f, but its core AC requires extracting and executing the LITERAL fenced commands from the README quickstart. If 0435 is implemented or merged before a41f fixes the session-log syntax and b55f repairs the quickstart structure, 0435 will extract and run the currently-broken README commands, permanently blocking CI until the siblings land.
findings.suggested_fix: Add 0435 to the dependency chain: a41f → b55f → 0435 (or at minimum after b55f), and document this in 0435's 'Files' or depends_on field.

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge the two 'Acceptance Criteria' sections into a single, deduplicated checklist so no obligation is silently dropped by an executor reading only one block.
findings.finding: The plan contains two separate '## Acceptance Criteria' sections with different content. An executing agent reading top-to-bottom may treat the first as the authoritative checklist and miss the expanded/more-specific second set (which adds the golden-path CI gate obligation, docs-index gate, and generated-references gate), or may be uncertain which set governs sign-off.
findings.location: Two '## Acceptance Criteria' headings — first after 'Success Criteria', second in the 'Drift-proofing mechanism' block
findings.suggested_fix: Combine both AC blocks under one '## Acceptance Criteria' heading, deduplicating overlapping items (e.g. 'every validated defect corrected' appears in both) and preserving all unique obligations from each block.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require b55f to document its chosen implementation path in a comment before 0435 is implemented, and have 0435 explicitly scope its fenced-block extraction to match whatever structure b55f produces.
findings.finding: Child b55f allows two mutually-exclusive implementation choices (genuine end-to-end vs. retitle/reframe), but child 0435 assumes the README quickstart fenced block will contain a runnable, coherent command sequence to extract and execute. If b55f chooses the retitle path and restructures the block into per-surface independent examples, 0435's fenced-block extraction logic may face ambiguity (which block to run? multiple disconnected blocks?), yet 0435 gives no guidance on how it handles this case.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a coordination note: b55f must resolve its design choice first and communicate the resulting fenced-block structure to 0435's implementer; alternatively, constrain b55f to the end-to-end option so 0435 can safely assume a single CLI fenced block.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a serialization order for the five children that edit `.github/workflows/test.yml` (e.g. e205 first to clear stale comments, then e866, 235a, f088, then 0435 last).
findings.finding: Five children (0435, e205, e866, 235a, f088) all modify `.github/workflows/test.yml` with no declared serialization order between them, unlike the root README.md stack which is explicitly serialized. This creates concurrent same-file edit risk analogous to the README conflict the parent explicitly solved.
findings.location: child 0435-8dce-4e9f-4121, child e205-cf65-aea8-42e9, child e866-0bdb-94e3-41e6, child 235a-e6c1-e034-4a75, child f088-ef68-9518-4833
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Shared-file edit ordering for test.yml' note to the parent plan mirroring the README stack, e.g. e205 → e866 → 235a → f088 → 0435, and have each child declare depends_on the prior test.yml editor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare a serialization order between 9d81 and 6755 for their shared docs/README.md edits (e.g. 9d81 restructures first, then 6755 adds router on top of the new structure).
findings.finding: Children 9d81 and 6755 both edit `docs/README.md` with no declared serialization between them — a same-file concurrent edit risk analogous to the root README.md stack the parent explicitly serializes.
findings.suggested_fix: Add 6755 depends_on 9d81, since the intent router's placement ('near the top') is more stable once the full restructuring from 9d81 is done.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete source artifacts (file paths or module entry-points) that constitute the 'live CLI registry' and 'live MCP registry' in this AC, or state a default (e.g. 'output of `rebar --help`' and 'MCP tool-list endpoint response') so the gate target is unambiguous.
findings.finding: 'Generated CLI + MCP references drift-gate against their live registries' does not name the concrete source artifacts that constitute the 'live registries', making it impossible for an executor to determine unambiguously whether this AC is satisfied.
findings.location: Second Acceptance Criteria block, bullet: 'Generated CLI + MCP references drift-gate against their live registries.'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the AC to read, for example: 'Generated CLI reference drift-gates against `rebar --help` output; generated MCP reference drift-gates against the live MCP tool-list response (mirroring the `gen_env_registry.py` pattern).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge the two '## Acceptance Criteria' sections into one authoritative list that includes all guards (refuted-config no-op, inventory deferral, golden-path gate, docs-index drift, generated CLI+MCP drift-gate).
findings.finding: The plan contains two separate '## Acceptance Criteria' sections with different, partially-overlapping content. The first (4 items) includes guards about the refuted config finding and deferring inventory work; the second (4 items) replaces and extends those with more precise drift-gate language. An implementer reading both would be uncertain which is authoritative, and two items in the first set ('refuted config-format finding not acted on', 'lower-value inventory work captured as idea') have no counterpart in the second set, creating unresolved gaps.
findings.location: Both '## Acceptance Criteria' sections (first after 'Success Criteria', second after 'Shared-file edit ordering')
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the first AC section and fold its unique items ('refuted config-format finding is NOT acted on', 'lower-value inventory work captured as idea, not shipped') into the second, more precise AC section as additional bullets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether the quickstart CI gate (a) replaces probe-rebar.sh with a README-extractor, (b) wraps probe-rebar.sh to extract README commands first, or (c) requires both — and update the Reuse note to reflect the chosen approach.
findings.finding: The 'Reuse note' implies the action for the quickstart CI gate is to wire `probe-rebar.sh` into CI ('it just is not wired into CI'), but the Drift-proofing mechanism section specifies a different mechanism: extract fenced command blocks from the README and execute those exact lines. These are two distinct implementations of the same gate; the plan does not resolve which takes precedence or whether both are required.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Reuse note: 'probe-rebar.sh is NOT the golden-path gate mechanism; the gate extracts and runs README fenced commands verbatim. probe-rebar.sh may be retired or repurposed once the new gate is green.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan section (or child ticket reference) that describes what the CLI and MCP generated references are (which source files, which output docs, which generator script, which CI gate command) so the 'Generated CLI + MCP references drift-gate' AC is verifiable.
findings.finding: The second AC set includes 'Generated CLI + MCP references drift-gate against their live registries' as a deliverable criterion, but the plan body describes only the env-registry generator pattern (`gen_env_registry.py`) as a proven template — no section describes the CLI or MCP reference generators, their output files, or how their drift gates would work. This criterion is partially orphaned.
findings.location: Second '## Acceptance Criteria' section, bullet 4; 'Reuse note' paragraph

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a child-ticket decomposition table to the plan (id, title, scope) or add stable links/references so the ticket identifiers in 'Shared-file edit ordering' resolve unambiguously.
findings.finding: The 'Shared-file edit ordering' section references six specific ticket shorthand identifiers (`a41f`, `b55f`, `319a`, `b5a4`, `e866`, `235a`) and one named ticket (`contained-dendritic-angora`/0435) as if they are defined child tickets, but the plan contains no ticket decomposition section defining these identifiers. The Success Criteria AC ('All child tickets are closed') also assumes child tickets exist and are enumerable, but they are not listed in the plan.
findings.location: 'Shared-file edit ordering' section; first AC set, bullet 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the disposition of `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` after the golden-path CI gate is introduced: either retire/remove it (with a note in the ticket), repurpose it as a local-dev convenience wrapper that calls the same extracted block, or explicitly note it is kept as-is and explain why it is not redundant.
findings.finding: After the golden-path CI gate is wired in (ticket contained-dendritic-angora/0435), `scripts/probe-rebar.sh` — which the plan acknowledges implements 'the full install→…→close golden path' with its own internally-correct syntax — has no documented fate. The plan neither retires it, updates it to serve a distinct role, nor notes it will stay as-is with an explanation. A reader or contributor encountering both the new CI gate and the script will find conflicting or redundant golden-path tooling, and the script may diverge silently from the README as it is no longer the authoritative path.
findings.location: Context / Reuse note: 'scripts/probe-rebar.sh already implements the full install→…→close golden path; it just is not wired into CI.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note in ticket contained-dendritic-angora/0435 (or its AC): 'probe-rebar.sh is kept as a local-dev convenience script; the CI gate is authoritative. A comment is added to the script header pointing to the CI gate as the source of truth.' If instead the script is superseded, add an AC item to delete or archive it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike or RED fixture for the README fenced-block extractor (ticket 0435): run it against the current README, assert it selects exactly the intended quickstart commands, and record the extraction rule (e.g., 'first ```bash block under the Quickstart heading') as the validated contract before building the full CI gate.
findings.finding: The README fenced-block extraction-and-execute mechanism (the core of the quickstart CI gate) is novel and its correctness is asserted without an empirical validation step. How exactly multi-line blocks, setup-only lines, env-var requirements, or interleaved prose are handled is unspecified, and there is no spike, RED fixture, or pilot run to validate the extractor before the full gate is built.
findings.suggested_fix: In ticket 0435, add an explicit spike step: run the proposed extractor against `origin/main` README, print the extracted lines, and manually verify they match the intended quickstart commands. Capture the extraction rule (heading anchor + fence type) in a one-line comment in the CI script so it's self-documenting and reviewable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CLAUDE.md or brief design-doc section (or inline README note in the scripts directory) documenting the drift-proofing architecture: what each gate does, which artifact it is authoritative for, and the extraction rule used by the quickstart CI gate.
findings.finding: The novel CI architecture — README-literal extraction gate and the gen_env_registry pattern applied to CLI/MCP references — is not captured in any ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design doc. Future maintainers have no documented explanation of why the gate exists, how the extraction works, or what the drift-gate contract is.
findings.location: Drift-proofing mechanism section / Success Criteria / AC block

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly acknowledge the CI→live-registry coupling for the generated-reference drift-gates: specify whether the registry is a local file artifact (safe) or a live service call, and if the latter, document the failure mode and whether CI should hard-fail or warn on registry unavailability.
findings.finding: The 'generated CLI + MCP references drift-gate against their live registries' introduces a new CI-to-live-registry coupling that is silently assumed to work without acknowledging the interface contract, failure modes, or how CI behaves when the registry is unavailable or mid-update.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Generated CLI + MCP references drift-gate against their live registries'
findings.suggested_fix: If the 'live registry' is a local generated file (like gen_env_registry.py produces), clarify that in the plan to confirm there is no network dependency. If it is a live service call, add a fallback/offline mode or convert to a local snapshot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that makes the taxonomy/audience-router goal falsifiable, e.g. 'docs/README.md contains a labelled audience-router section and each entry is tagged living-reference or historical-evidence; a reviewer checklist item or automated label-presence check confirms this at merge.'
findings.finding: The 'clarify taxonomy' goal (audience-intent router, separation of living reference from historical evidence) is named in Context and Success Criteria but has no Acceptance Criterion that makes it falsifiable or verifiable at ticket close.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'The docs index is complete and clearly separates living reference from historical evidence, with an intent-based router for the four audiences.' / Acceptance Criteria section (no corresponding AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: '- [ ] `docs/README.md` contains an explicit audience-router section (e.g. "Start here by role") and each listed document is labelled as living-reference or historical-evidence; confirmed by reviewer checklist at PR merge for the docs-index ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate a final integration step (e.g. in child b5a4 as the last-to-merge ticket, or as a separate integration note in the epic) that explicitly runs make lint && make typecheck && make test after all children have landed.
findings.finding: No child explicitly owns the aggregate integration verification: parent AC 'make lint && make typecheck && make test pass on the aggregate.' Each child ensures its own CI passes, but no child is scoped to run the full suite across all landed changes together and confirm no cross-child regression.
findings.location: parent acceptance criteria (first block, item B)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to b5a4 (the last ticket in the dependency chain) an AC: 'make lint && make typecheck && make test pass with all sibling changes landed,' since b5a4 already depends_on e866 and 235a and is last in the README stack.

findings.finding: Ticket 6755's 'symptom->fix troubleshooting table' deliverable is silently absent from the plan — the plan describes only the intent-based router half of that ticket.

The plan's chosen CI drift-proofing mechanism for 'quickstart-command rot' (wiring probe-rebar.sh into CI) validates command EXECUTION correctness but does not prevent the README quickstart TEXT from drifting — the two surfaces are independent. The probe uses dynamically-generated ticket IDs and internally-correct subcommand syntax; it cannot detect that the README still shows `rebar session-log "<entry>"` (missing `append` verb) or a stale alias. The named goal ('make quickstart-command rot fail the build automatically') requires a mechanism that checks README text accuracy, not just command executability.
findings.location: Success Criteria / Reuse note — 'New CI gates make quickstart-command rot … fail the build automatically' + 'scripts/probe-rebar.sh already implements the full install→…→close golden path; it just is not wired into CI.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small CI step (or pytest parametrize) that extracts the bash code blocks from README.md's Quickstart section and validates: (1) each `rebar <subcommand>` matches a known subcommand from `rebar --help`, (2) `mcp>=X.Y` in the prose matches the floor in pyproject.toml. This complements probe-wired CI (which catches runtime breakage) and closes the text-drift gap.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
